title_en: Why are big AI labs hiring so many philosophers?
title_zh: 为什么大型人工智能实验室雇佣这么多哲学家？
rubric_en: Also this week, the impact of the Iran war, academic standards, tall men, Alan Greenspan, Warrington
rubric_zh: 此外本周我们还关注伊朗战争、学术标准、身高、阿兰·格林斯潘和沃灵顿

paragraphs.en: Letters are welcome via email to letters@economist.comFind out more about how we process your letter
paragraphs.zh: 欢迎通过电子邮件向 letters@economist.com 投稿投稿。了解我们处理来信的方式。

paragraphs.en: You reported on why big artificial-intelligence labs are hiring so many philosophers and noted its impact on the ethics of war (“Computo, ergo sum”, June 27th). Philosophers excel at formulating questions, but they have no agreed answers to how military objectives should be weighed against civilian deaths. For example, if a military objective of killing a wartime leader in a foreign country can be achieved only at the expense of the deaths of innocent civilians, what is the maximum number of such deaths that is acceptable? This number, known as the non-combatant casualty cut-off value (NCV), is often specified by military authorities but has varied widely (for example, between 0 and 30) across campaigns and over time. Its variability betrays the uncertainty of those who set it.
paragraphs.zh: 你曾报道过大型人工智能实验室为何雇佣大量哲学家，并指出这对战争伦理学的影响（《Computo, ergo sum》，6月27日）。哲学家擅长提出问题，但并未就军事实体目标应如何与平民死亡进行权衡达成一致的答案。比如，如果达到在外国杀害一名战时领导人的军事目标只能以无辜平民死亡为代价，那么最多可接受多少此类死亡？这一数字——即非战斗人员伤亡上限值（NCV）——通常由军事当局规定，但在不同战役和不同时期差异很大（例如可在0到30之间）。这种变动反而暴露了制定者本身的不确定性。

paragraphs.en: There is no “correct” NCV for human judges or for AI systems. If we knew how many future deaths a wartime leader would be responsible for the problem would resolve itself into the well-known trolley dilemma. But we don’t know that. And even if we did, there is no universally acceptable solution to that dilemma.
paragraphs.zh: 对于人类法官而言，没有“正确”的NCV，也没有适用于人工智能系统的“正确”NCV。若我们知道一位战时领导人将来会导致多少人死亡，这个问题就会化归为著名的“电车难题”。但我们并不知道这一点。即便知道了，也没有一个普遍可接受的解答能够解决这一困境。

paragraphs.en: Nigel HarveyProfessor of judgment and decision researchUniversity College London
paragraphs.zh: 尼格尔·哈维，伦敦大学学院 判断与决策研究教授

paragraphs.en: “Cancelled penalty” (June 27th) was correct in its central contention about America’s waiver of sanctions on Iranian oil. It is a huge concession. But what looks significant on paper—an apparent aberration against more than 40 years of sanctions pressure—is decidedly less so in reality. The twin pressures of European and British sanctions will hold back any potential flood of Western buyers. Instead, the waiver will chiefly facilitate Iranian state actors’ exports to pre-existing trading partners.
paragraphs.zh: 《取消处罚》（6月27日）在其关于美国对伊朗石油制裁豁免的核心观点上是正确的。这是一个巨大的让步。但在纸面上看似重大的内容——看起来是对四十多年制裁压力的明显例外——在现实中却要温和得多。欧洲和英国制裁的双重压力将遏制任何潜在的西方买家洪流。相反，豁免主要会促进伊朗国家行为体向既有贸易伙伴的出口。

paragraphs.en: Then there is the prospect of the Strait of Hormuz being closed again with comparative ease, which still lingers over the global economy. All the while, it is entirely possible that Iran continues to build up its nuclear capability.
paragraphs.zh: 此外，霍尔木兹海峡再次被轻易关闭的可能性仍在全球经济上空萦绕。与此同时，伊朗持续增强其核能力的可能性也完全存在。

paragraphs.en: What could mark a break is if alternative trading routes that bypass the strait were to be established, which could check Iranian leverage. The big question here is not only how quickly this can be achieved, but whether the attendant legal and political agreements can be reached. For all the talk of waivers and sanctions, this is the dime on which the global economy might turn in the future.
paragraphs.zh: 如果建立绕过该海峡的替代贸易路线，从而削弱伊朗的影响力，这才可能形成转折点。这里的大问题不仅是能否快速实现，更在于随之而来的法律和政治协议是否能够达成。尽管大家都在谈论豁免和制裁，但这才是未来全球经济可能转向的关键所在。

paragraphs.en: James Willn Reed SmithDubai
paragraphs.zh: 詹姆斯·威尔姆·里德·史密斯，迪拜

paragraphs.en: The Iran war is indeed the third shock in a decade to hit Africa after covid-19 and Russia’s invasion of Ukraine (“Third time unlucky”, June 20th). But the deeper problem is that many African economies still lack quick, affordable financing when such shocks arrive.
paragraphs.zh: 伊朗战争确实是十年来冲击非洲的第三次震荡，继新冠肺炎疫情和俄罗斯入侵乌克兰之后（“Third time unlucky”，2026年6月20日）。但更深层的问题在于，许多非洲经济体在此类冲击到来时仍然缺乏快速且负担得起的融资。

paragraphs.en: Kenya illustrates this. Reserves are being rapidly eroded and fuel protests have turned deadly, yet the government’s main recourse is slow; a fresh $600m loan from the World Bank now and probably an IMF facility later. Full IMF arrangements typically involve months of negotiation over conditions. That is time a country in a live currency crisis can least afford. Kenya is far from alone in this bind. Egypt, Ethiopia, Mozambique and Rwanda also face the same choice between burning through reserves and waiting on a negotiated rescue.
paragraphs.zh: 肯尼亚说明了这一点。外汇储备正在快速被侵蚀，燃料抗议已酿成死亡事件，而政府的主要应对却很慢；现在只能靠世界银行一笔新的6亿美元贷款，而后续可能再求助于IMF融资工具。完整的IMF安排通常要经过数月的条件谈判。对于正陷入货币危机的国家而言，这恰恰是最无法承受的时间。肯尼亚并非孤例。埃及、埃塞俄比亚、莫桑比克和卢旺达也面临同样的两难：要么烧掉储备，要么等待谈判援助。

paragraphs.en: The architecture for faster support already exists and has worked for Africa before. In September 2022 the IMF opened a temporary food-shock window, which let countries hit by the Ukraine war’s effect on grain and fertiliser prices borrow on top of normal limits, with minimal conditionality and no lengthy programme required. By the time it closed in mid-2023 it had disbursed $1.8bn across six countries, including Burkina Faso, Guinea, Malawi and South Sudan.
paragraphs.zh: 更快的援助架构其实已经存在，并且此前在非洲已有成效。2022年9月，IMF开设了一个临时粮食冲击窗口，允许受乌克兰战争对粮食和肥料价格冲击影响的国家，在常规额度之上借款，且附带条件很少，不需要冗长的项目安排。到2023年年中结束时，它已向六个国家发放了18亿美元，包括布基纳法索、几内亚、马拉维和南苏丹。

paragraphs.en: That window should be reopened and adapted into an oil-shock window for net energy importers exposed to the shocks from the Iran war, with comparable access limits and the same rapid disbursement. Although this would not solve Africa’s underlying debt burden, it would recognise that liquidity, not just solvency, determines whether a price shock becomes a currency crisis.
paragraphs.zh: 这个窗口应当重新开启，并改为服务于受伊朗战争冲击、且净能源进口国的石油冲击窗口，提供与之相当的可及额度和同样快速的拨付。尽管这不能解决非洲的根本性债务负担，但它能承认这样一个事实：是流动性而不仅是偿付能力，决定着价格冲击是否会演变为货币危机。

paragraphs.en: Faith NyabutoCambridge
paragraphs.zh: Faith Nyabuto，剑桥

paragraphs.en: Your leader on falling academic standards (“Don’t dumb down”, June 27th) rightly noted that pushing young people up the educational ladder without the skills to match helps no one and is, at its core, cowardly. Yet the challenge of cheating posed by AI may carry its own corrective.
paragraphs.zh: 你在《学术标准在下滑》（《别故意降格》）一文中于6月27日发出的观点是正确的：把年轻人往高学位上推升，却没有相应的能力作支撑，对任何人都无益，其根本是软弱的做法。然而，生成式人工智能带来的学术作弊问题，可能会带来一种纠偏。

paragraphs.en: By collapsing the cost of fabricated coursework, generative AI confronts every university with a blunt choice: pay for much costlier enforcement, or in effect abandon the pretence of academic integrity. Institutions with thin defences and less academic intakes will rationally give up, lowering fees and operating openly as credential mills. Those with serious invigilation and abler students will instead double down, as Harvard did recently by capping the number of A grades handed out in each course to roughly 20% of enrolled students (the policy comes into effect next year). Colleges of this type will still charge a premium for a degree that still certifies genuine learning. This would result in what economists call a separating equilibrium, the market sorting what administrators cannot.
paragraphs.zh: 生成式人工智能把伪造课程作业的成本几乎降到了零，使每所大学都面临一个残酷选择：要么支付高得多的监管执行成本，要么在事实上放弃“学术诚信”这件事。防线薄弱、招生门槛较低的学校，理性上会放弃这套要求，降低学费并公开把自己当作“文凭工厂”运作。反而是那些有着严格监考并且学生质量更高的学校，会加倍加码——哈佛最近就是这么做的，它将每门课程的A档成绩人数限制在约20%的注册学生，且该政策将于明年生效。这类学校仍会对仍然能证明真实学习的文凭收取更高学费。这将形成经济学里所谓的“分离均衡（separating equilibrium）”，即市场会按层级筛选出管理层无法亲自完成的区分。

paragraphs.en: This need not be lamented. For the many jobs that reward reliability and persistence above any particular syllabus, a degree was always mostly a signal. An honest credential may serve its holder better, and far more cheaply, than one that pretends otherwise.
paragraphs.zh: 这并不一定值得哀叹。对于多数依赖可靠性和持续性胜过某一门课程大纲内容来评估的工作岗位来说，学位本质上一直更像是一种信号。真正诚实的资质认定，可能比那种假装如此的文凭，既更能帮助持有者，也更省成本。

paragraphs.en: Peter DonahueHouston
paragraphs.zh: 彼得·多纳休，休斯敦

paragraphs.en: Degree standards are experiencing astonishing grade inflation. About 30% of students now achieve a first-class degree in Britain. In the 1960s, usually only one or two students would achieve this elusive prize, and sometimes none at all.
paragraphs.zh: 英国的学位标准正出现令人震惊的“成绩膨胀”。如今约有30%的学生在英国获得一等学位。20世纪60年代，这个难得的荣誉通常只有一两名学生能拿到，甚至有时一个也没有。

paragraphs.en: Policymakers should indeed “widen the ways in which people can continue learning after they leave school”. As adult education is a permissive rather than statutory level of education the starving of local government funding has caused the shredding of the once valuable network of adult centres with their attractive courses across so many subjects. Now the only service in many areas is the excellent University of the Third Age, which utilises voluntary pro bono teachers (such as myself) in classes available at a small subscription fee. One hopes that in this latter area at least the u3a model can be used as a cost-effective provision in what is at present a wilderness.
paragraphs.zh: 政策制定者确实应当“拓宽人们离开学校后继续学习的渠道”。但成人教育是“允许性”而非“法定性”的教育层次，地方政府经费匮乏导致遍布众多学科的成人学习中心这一曾经宝贵的网络几近瓦解。如今在许多地区，唯一可见的服务就是表现优异的“第三年龄大学”（University of the Third Age），它在每门课程中以极低订阅费的方式组织课程，由志愿无偿教师（例如我自己）授课。人们至少可以希望，在这一领域里，现阶段仍是“教育荒原”之际，u3a 模式至少能作为一种更具成本效益的供给方式得到应用。

paragraphs.en: Bill JonesDirector of extra-mural studies at Manchester University, 1986-93Beverley, East Yorkshire
paragraphs.zh: 比尔·琼斯，曼彻斯特大学继续教育（拓展课程）主管，1986—1993；贝弗利，东约克郡

paragraphs.en: Tall men earn more, date more and are generally happier all round, you say (Graphic Detail, June 19th). As a six-foot, four-inch male I would like to point out that it’s not all a bed of roses. The world is designed by short people for short people. Your privileged tall man is crushed into small aircraft seats and bangs his head on low doorways. Hotel beds are often too short. There have been times in my life when I have had to order shoes from abroad. And then there’s clothing and pants that finish at mid-calf. There seems to be a convention in clothing stores that all large and extra-large sizes are placed on the bottom shelves, so the short king will find his size at eye level. He doesn’t even need to remove his crown, requiring tall men. On the other hand, extra large sizes are stacked at floor level and the tall emperor is required requiring tall men to get on hands and knees to retrieve an article.
paragraphs.zh: 长得高的人通常赚得更多、约会更多、总体上也更快乐，你这么说很对（《Graphic Detail》，6月19日）。作为一名身高六英尺四英寸的男性，我想指出，这并不完全是风光大道。这个世界是按矮个子为矮个子设计的。你这个特权阶层中的高个子会被挤进小型飞机座位，低矮的门框也常常让他撞到脑袋。酒店床往往太短。我的人生中有些时候甚至得从国外订鞋子。再加上裤子等衣服常常只做到小腿中部。服装店里似乎有个默认：所有大号和加大号都摆在下层货架上，所以这个“矮王”能在眼平高度就找到自己的尺码——他甚至不必摘掉王冠，就能满足对高个子的需求。另一方面，加大码又被堆在地面高度附近，而“高个皇帝”却必须用手和膝盖爬着去拿一件衣物。

paragraphs.en: So it does seem an act of celestial justice that we score better with the ladies.
paragraphs.zh: 因此我们在女性那里更受欢迎，似乎确实算是天意的公平回报。

paragraphs.en: Spot-on as your June 27th obituary of Alan Greenspan was, it might have added a detail pertinent to The Economist. Economics coverage in the paper in the 1960s relied heavily on a single resident genius, Norman Macrae. Clandestine extra input to both Macrae and coverage of the United States was supplied by Alan Greenspan. Each week a junior in the Washington office would make two telephone calls to New York: one was to Henry Kaufman, chief economist at Salomon Brothers; the second was to Greenspan, then main partner of Townsend Greenspan.
paragraphs.zh: 你在6月27日写的艾伦·格林斯潘讣告一针见血；如果其中再补一句与《The Economist》相关的细节，也许更为贴切。该报记者在1960年代的经济报道在很大程度上依赖于一位驻地天才——诺曼·麦克雷。每周华盛顿办公室的一位初级人员都会打两个电话到纽约：一个打给索洛蒙兄弟公司的首席经济学家亨利·考夫曼；另一个打给当时还是汤森德·格林斯潘（Townsend Greenspan）核心合伙人的艾伦·格林斯潘。

paragraphs.en: His dry wit, analysis and parsing of often obscure labour and stock level statistics much deepened The Economist’s columns at the time.
paragraphs.zh: 他那干练的诙谐、透彻分析和对常常晦涩的劳工与库存统计数据的拆解，大大丰富了当时《The Economist》的专栏。

paragraphs.en: Andrew KnightEditor of The Economist, 1974-86Shipston-on-Stour, Warwickshire
paragraphs.zh: 安德鲁·奈特，《经济学人》前编辑（1974-1986年），沃里克郡谢普斯顿-奥恩-斯托（Shipston-on-Stour）

paragraphs.en: “Warrington is not a glamorous place”, as you acknowledge in your article on the economic miracle of this town in northern England (“Meet the Warrington model”, June 27th). You mention the Royal Society of Arts ranking the town last out of 325 places for cultural amenities (yes, even behind Luton).
paragraphs.zh: 你在题为《沃灵顿模式》（“Meet the Warrington model”，6月27日）的文章中承认，“沃灵顿并不是一个光鲜亮丽的地方”。你还提到皇家艺术学会将该城在325个地区的文化设施排名中列在最后（是的，甚至输给了卢顿）。

paragraphs.en: As Eric Turner, a local artist who’s been called Warrington’s secret Lowry, would have said, “Rubbish…have they ever been to Widnes?”
paragraphs.zh: 正如当地艺术家埃里克·特纳（Eric Turner）——人称“沃灵顿的秘密洛里”——会说的：“胡说八道……他们到底去过温德斯（Widnes）吗？”

paragraphs.en: Does anyone remember the contest from some years back? First prize: one week in Warrington. Second prize: two weeks in Warrington.
paragraphs.zh: 你还记得几年前那场比赛吗？一等奖：在沃灵顿住一周。二等奖：在沃灵顿住两周。
